description: Colorado wins series 4-1 vs Minnesota Wild
thesis: market overreacted to the Game 4 win and is now pricing the sweep too high at 61c YES. Your model says COL wins any single game ~56%, meaning they DON'T clinch in Game 5 about 44% of the time. Market says only 36%.
close_note: NO on "COL wins 4-1" — meaning you were betting the series would NOT end exactly 4-1. It did end 4-1. That's a loss. The YES side resolves at 100c, your NO at 36c resolves at 0c.

description: Montreal wins series 4-2 vs Buffalo Sabres
thesis: Montreal leads 2-1 with Dobes playing elite hockey, but series exactness markets are historically overpriced. The model gives 83% probability the series does NOT end exactly 4-2 — it either goes shorter (MTL wins 4-0 or 4-1) or longer (4-3). Buffalo's Lyon is solid, Tage Thompson is one hot streak away from taking a game, and Montreal has alternated wins and losses all playoffs. Market is pricing the 4-2 outcome at 66c YES when it should be closer to 17c.
close_note: "Position oversized — 73 contracts at 70c exceeds $25 max loss limit. Closing to maintain data integrity. Thesis valid, execution price and sizing were wrong.
